summary: The build failed during C++ compilation: explicit instantiation of operator() in the SIMD moments code produced errors, stopping the meson/ninja build and causing packaging metadata generation to fail.
root_cause: C++ compilation error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' in the SIMD moments sources does not refer to a valid function template/member, causing the compiler to fail.

evidence.quote: ../../pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp:255:50: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: Direct compiler error pointing to an invalid explicit instantiation in moments_simd.hpp

evidence.quote: ../../pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp:14:43: error: explicit instantiation of 'operator()' does not refer to a function template, variable template, member function, member class, or static data member
evidence.reason: The same explicit-instantiation error is reported from the NEON instantiation source, confirming the instantiation site is problematic
The compilation of the NEON object file failed (nonzero exit code)
The compiler emitted an error and stopped compilation
The ninja build halted due to the compilation failure
The packaging step failed because the build/subprocess exited with an error

suggested_fix: Inspect the explicit instantiation at the locations reported (e.g. '../../pandas/_libs/include/pandas/simd/moments_simd.hpp' line 255 and '../../pandas/_libs/simd/moments_inst_neon.cpp' line 14). Ensure that operator() is a function template/member that can be explicitly instantiated, or remove/correct the invalid explicit instantiation; then re-run the meson/ninja build.
